mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 2.5% (CPIAUCSL) triggers central bank rate hikes, increasing discount rates and reducing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 245 despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in FEDFUNDS and rising CPIAUCSL indicate accommodative policy amid inflation, creating uncertainty that depresses equity returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX shows positive 20d returns in > 50% of cases where both FEDFUNDS < 1.0 and CPIAUCSL > 244.

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5pp monthly decline in UNRATE signals tightening labor markets, increasing wage pressures and corporate costs, which reduces S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of instances where UNRATE declines by >0.5pp monthly.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) reflects stronger manufacturing activity, boosting corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX shows negative 20d returns in > 40% of periods where INDPRO rises by >1% monthly.

mechanism_narrative: Positive PAYEMS growth combined with declining UNRATE indicates a strong labor market, increasing consumer spending and driving S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 50% of cases where PAYEMS > 100k and UNRATE < 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in housing starts (HOUST) and real sales (RSAFS) signals strong consumer demand, boosting corporate revenues and driving S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX shows negative 20d returns in > 40% of instances where both HOUST > 1200k and RSAFS > 470k.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP growth (GDPC1) above 2% indicates robust economic expansion, increasing corporate profits and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 30% of quarters where GDPC1 exceeds 2% growth.
